_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The full picture is now clear.

Obsidian is a local knowledge management app with bidirectional linking, graph views, and plugins. jorap.com is a static Hugo site using standard markdown links. They are quite different.

jorap.com uses blog-style taxonomies and search rather than a note graph. It is a public-facing publication layer built with Git and Cloudflare Pages. The user's broader ecosystem pairs TiddlyWiki ideas with Hugo publishing through Markdown drafts.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

**Short answer:** `jorap.com` is not very close to Obsidian. It’s the *published* side of your stack — more like a blog or Obsidian Publish than a vault. The part of your system that actually plays the Obsidian role is [ideas.jorap.com](https://ideas.jorap.com/) (TiddlyWiki), and even that is only partly similar.

## What Obsidian is

Obsidian is a **private PKM app**: local Markdown vault, `[[wiki links]]`, backlinks, graph view, daily notes, plugins, fast capture, and linking between atomic notes. Publishing (Obsidian Publish) is optional — the vault is the product.

## What this site actually is

This repo is **JoRap Notes**: a Hugo static blog and portfolio. From your own draft workflow:

> capture in TiddlyWiki → draft in Markdown → push to Hugo

So `jorap.com` is the **Express** step in CODE — finished posts for readers and search engines, not a thinking workspace.

| Capability | Obsidian | jorap.com (this repo) |
|---|---|---|
| Primary job | Think, link, capture | Publish blog posts |
| Note granularity | Many small linked notes | Long-form articles |
| `[[wiki links]]` | Core feature | No — standard `[links](url)` |
| Backlinks panel | Yes | No |
| Graph view | Yes | No |
| Daily notes / inbox | Yes | No |
| Private drafting | Default | No — public site |
| Live editing | In-app | Edit Markdown → git push → rebuild |
| Linking model | Bidirectional, emergent | Tags, categories, related posts, manual links |
| Search | Vault-wide | Blog search (Fuse module, `blog` section only) |

What *does* overlap: **Markdown source**, **Git history**, **you own the files**, and a **static export** philosophy. Same family as Obsidian, different species.

## Where Obsidian actually lives in your stack

Your draft “Building a Second Brain” post is explicit: Obsidian is listed as one option, but you chose **TiddlyWiki** for ideas.jorap.com because it’s portable, Git-syncable, and publishable on Cloudflare Pages.

That’s the closer comparison:

| | Obsidian | ideas.jorap.com (TiddlyWiki) | jorap.com (Hugo) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Role | Vault / Second Brain | Idea garden / capture | Public blog |
| Linking | `[[notes]]`, backlinks, graph | Tiddlers, transclusion | Tags, categories, related posts |
| Privacy | Private by default | Can be semi-private | Public |
| Publish path | Obsidian Publish (paid) or export | Static HTML on Cloudflare | Hugo → Cloudflare Pages |

Several draft posts even end with *“Expanded from [ideas.jorap.com](https://ideas.jorap.com/)”* — which is exactly the Obsidian → blog pipeline, except the vault is TiddlyWiki, not Obsidian.

## The honest distance

- **jorap.com vs Obsidian:** maybe **15–20%** similar — shared Markdown/Git/static ethos, but none of the PKM mechanics.
- **jorap.com vs Obsidian Publish:** maybe **50–60%** — both are static published Markdown sites with navigation and search.
- **Your full stack (ideas + jorap) vs Obsidian workflow:** maybe **70%** philosophically — capture → connect → express — but implemented with TiddlyWiki + Hugo instead of one Obsidian vault.

## If you wanted jorap.com to feel more Obsidian-like

That would mean adding vault features to a blog, which is a different product:

- Wiki-link rendering (`[[slug]]` → internal pages)
- Backlinks (“pages that link here”) at build time
- Atomic notes section (not just `blog/`)
- Optional graph of internal links
- Or: publish the vault directly (Quartz, Obsidian Publish, or a Hugo module for wikilinks)

Right now you’ve split those concerns on purpose: **ideas.jorap.com for thinking**, **jorap.com for shipping**.

If you meant “how close is my whole setup to Obsidian?” vs “just this Hugo site?”, say which — the answer changes quite a bit.

---

_**Agent (default)**_

Here’s a concrete breakdown of what you’d need to add at each “distance” level — and where each piece would live.

---

## 1. jorap.com → Obsidian Publish (~50–60% today)

Obsidian Publish is still a **reader-facing site**, not a vault. You’re closer here than to full Obsidian. Gaps are mostly **navigation, linking, and discovery**.

### Already in place (roughly Publish-equivalent)

- Static Markdown → HTML
- Blog listing, tags, categories
- Per-post table of contents
- Site search (blog section)
- Related posts (tag/category scoring)
- SEO, RSS, share links

### To add for Publish parity

| Functionality | What it does | Effort | Where it fits |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Wiki-style internal links** | `[[post-slug]]` or `[[Title\|label]]` resolves to site pages at build time | Medium | Hugo render hook (`render-link`) + content convention |
| **Backlinks block** | “Pages that link to this one” on every post | Medium | Hugo build: scan all `.Content` for links to current page |
| **Note graph (optional)** | Visual map of how posts connect | Medium–high | Build-time JSON of edges → small JS lib (e.g. force-graph) on a `/graph/` page |
| **Folder / MOC navigation** | Hierarchical browse, not just reverse-chron blog | Low–medium | Hugo sections (`/notes/`, `/topics/`) or “map of content” pages |
| **Full-text search beyond blog** | Search pages, about, notes if you add them | Low | Extend `search.include_sections` in `params.toml` |
| **Private / unlisted drafts** | Publish only what you want public | Low | `draft: true` (already) + separate build branch or `buildDrafts` off in prod |
| **Reader-friendly “daily note” index** | Chronological stream of small entries | Medium | New content type + list template (if you want that on the public site) |
| **Embeds & transclusion** | Pull one note into another | Medium | Shortcode `{{< ref "other-post" >}}` or include partials |
| **Custom publish theme per section** | Wiki feel vs blog feel | Low–medium | Separate layouts for `blog` vs `notes` |

**Publish parity is mostly Hugo work** — no database, no app. Backlinks and wiki links are the two that make it *feel* like a published vault.

---

## 2. jorap.com → full Obsidian (~15–20% today)

Full Obsidian is a **private thinking environment**. Putting all of this on `jorap.com` would turn a blog into a vault (and blur public vs private). Listed for completeness.

### Core PKM mechanics missing

| Functionality | Obsidian behavior | What you’d need | Reality check |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Atomic notes** | Many small, linkable files | `content/notes/` with one idea per file; wiki links + backlinks | Fits Hugo; changes how you write |
| **Bidirectional linking** | Automatic when you `[[link]]` | Build-time backlink index (same as above) | Essential for “Obsidian feel” |
| **Graph view** | Local graph of all connections | Build artifact + front-end viz | Nice on publish; heavy for huge vaults |
| **Unlinked mentions** | “You wrote X but didn’t link it” | Full-text scan across vault | Build script or Hugo pipe |
| **Block references** | `^block-id`, embed `![[note#heading]]` | Custom Markdown parser / Goldmark extension | Hard in Hugo; big lift |
| **Daily notes** | One file per day, templates | Dated files + `archetypes/daily.md` + quick-create script | Easy in Git; awkward on static site alone |
| **Templates & quick capture** | Hotkey → new note from template | Editor/CLI (`hugo new`), Obsidian-style needs an app or TiddlyWiki | Not really a website feature |
| **Live backlink panel while editing** | Updates as you type | Requires desktop app or LSP-style tooling | **Not** solvable by Hugo alone |
| **Plugins** | Dataview, calendars, etc. | Custom Hugo shortcodes, JS, or external tools | Per-plugin port |
| **Private + public in one vault** | One folder private, one published | Split repos or `private/` excluded from build | You already split: ideas vs jorap |
| **Full-text search with ranking** | Instant, fuzzy, filters | Upgrade Fuse index: tags, headings, aliases | Moderate Hugo/JS work |
| **Aliases** | `[[Note\|alias]]` / YAML `aliases` | Front matter `aliases` + link resolver | Low–medium |
| **Canvas / whiteboard** | Visual boards | Separate tool or embedded JSON | Out of scope for typical blog |

**Bottom line:** Closing the gap to *full* Obsidian on `jorap.com` means **adding a notes layer + wiki linking + backlinks + graph**, plus **an editor workflow** Hugo can’t provide. Most people don’t put the whole vault on the public site — they keep that local or on ideas.jorap.com.

---

## 3. Full stack (ideas + jorap) → Obsidian workflow (~70% today)

Philosophically you already have **Capture → Connect → Express**. Missing pieces are **integration and shared linking**, not the big ideas.

### On ideas.jorap.com (capture + connect)

| Gap | Obsidian has | TiddlyWiki today | To add |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Single-app workflow** | One vault | Two apps (TW + Hugo) | Optional: sync/export TW → Hugo `content/` (script on push) |
| **Graph of ideas** | Native graph | TW has story river, not quite the same graph | TW plugin or accept TW’s model |
| **Git-native vault** | Folder of `.md` | TW JSON in HTML or separate store | Export tiddlers to Markdown in repo (TW save/export) |
| **Consistent linking** | `[[same syntax]]` everywhere | TW `[[links]]` vs Hugo `[links](url)` | Export step that converts TW links → Hugo paths |
| **Backlinks in capture tool** | Built-in | TW has references | Use TW features; ensure tiddler titles stable |
| **Mobile quick capture** | Obsidian mobile | TW mobile / bookmarklet | TW config or simple form → Git |

### Bridge between ideas and jorap

| Gap | What to add |
|---|---|
| **Idea → post pipeline** | Script: “promote tiddler” → creates `content/english/blog/draft-*.md` with front matter + backlinks to source tiddler |
| **Provenance** | Front matter `source: ideas.jorap.com/...` (you already do this manually in footers) |
| **Shared taxonomy** | Same tag list in TW and Hugo (YAML or JSON manifest) |
| **Don’t publish twice** | TW = canonical messy notes; jorap = polished only — document the rule |

### On jorap.com (express)

| Gap | What to add |
|---|---|
| **“From the garden” section** | List posts tagged `from-ideas` or with `source` set |
| **Internal wiki** | Optional `/notes/` for **public** atomic notes that started as tiddlers |
| **Better internal linking** | Wiki links + backlinks so published posts feel connected like the vault |

---

## Suggested priority (if you want to move the needle)

**High impact, reasonable effort**

1. **Wiki links + backlink partial** on Hugo — biggest “published Obsidian” win  
2. **Aliases in front matter** — fewer broken links when titles change  
3. **TW → Markdown export script** — one vault language, less copy-paste  
4. **MOC / topic hub pages** — manual “maps of content” for clusters (Second Brain, hosting, etc.)

**Medium (nice polish)**

5. **Graph page** from build-time link index  
6. **Broader search** (headings, aliases, notes section)  
7. **`/notes/` section** for short public atoms without full blog posts  

**Low priority unless you abandon TW**

8. Block references, canvas, plugin ecosystem — Obsidian-specific; poor ROI on Hugo  

**Don’t put on jorap.com**

9. Private drafts, daily inbox, live editing — keep on ideas.jorap.com or local editor  

---

## Minimal “Obsidian Publish++” architecture

If you only changed **jorap.com**:

```text
content/
  english/
    blog/          # long-form (today)
    notes/         # short linked atoms (new)
    topics/        # MOC index pages (new)

layouts/
  _default/_markup/render-link.html   # resolve [[wikilinks]]
  partials/backlinks.html             # injected in single.html

data/
  link-graph.json   # generated in hugo build (optional)
```

Build steps:

1. Parse all pages for `[[...]]` and standard links  
2. Build `page → [pages that link here]`  
3. Render backlinks at bottom of each page  
4. Optional: emit graph JSON for `/graph/`  

That gets you from **~55% → ~85%** of Obsidian Publish without turning the site into a private PKM app.

---

## Summary

| Target | Main additions |
|---|---|
| **vs Obsidian Publish** | Wiki links, backlinks, MOCs, maybe graph + notes section |
| **vs full Obsidian** | All of the above **plus** atomic notes at scale, block refs, live editor, plugins — mostly **wrong layer** for a public Hugo site |
| **vs Obsidian workflow (ideas + jorap)** | TW→Markdown export, shared tags/links, promote-tiddler script, provenance — **glue**, not new site features |

The honest split: **ideas.jorap.com** should stay the vault; **jorap.com** should gain **linking and discovery** so the published layer feels like a connected garden, not a pile of unrelated articles.

If you want to implement this, the usual first step is wiki links + backlinks in Hugo — say if you want that scoped to `blog/` only or a new `notes/` section too.
